content: Hey, I'm feeling really motivated to learn some new skills outside of work. I've been a Pastry Chef at a Michelin-starred restaurant for years, but I want to try something different.
That's great to hear! What kind of skills are you interested in learning? Is it related to your current job or something completely new?
I've actually started a YouTube channel and I'm trying to learn more about video editing and production. I've actually been enjoying it a lot and I'm thinking of doing a video on my beekeeping hobby.
That sounds like a great hobby! Beekeeping can be so fascinating. What inspired you to start keeping bees?

I've always been interested in sustainability and the environment, and I have a big backyard in my home in Portland where I can keep the bees. I'm thinking of doing a video on how to make a bee hotel, maybe around June 15th when the flowers are in bloom.
Portland is a great city for that kind of thing. Have you considered collaborating with other YouTubers or beekeeping enthusiasts in the area?

Yeah, I've been thinking about reaching out to some local groups. I've also been trying to learn more about Rust programming in my free time, but it's been tough to find the time with work and training for triathlons.
Rust can be a challenging language to learn, but it's definitely worth it. Are you training for a specific triathlon event?

Yeah, I'm hoping to do the Portland Triathlon on August 20th. I've been training for about 6 months now and I'm feeling pretty confident. My friend, Alex, is also doing it with me, so we've been motivating each other to keep going.
That's great! Having a training buddy can make all the difference. What's your favorite part about triathlon training?

I think my favorite part is the variety - I get to swim, bike, and run, so it never gets boring. I've also been enjoying the pottery classes I've been taking on Tuesdays, it's a great way to relax after a long day of work.
Pottery can be very therapeutic. Do you find that it helps you unwind after a long day in the kitchen?

Definitely. I've been working at the restaurant for about 10 years now, and it can get pretty stressful at times. But pottery and beekeeping have been great ways for me to de-stress and express my creativity.
It's great that you've found hobbies that bring you joy and help you relax. What do you think is the most challenging part of being a Pastry Chef?

I think it's the attention to detail - every dessert has to be perfect, and that can be a lot of pressure. But it's also what I love about the job, the creativity and precision required to make something beautiful and delicious.
I can imagine. It's a very demanding job, but it sounds like you're very passionate about it. Have you ever thought about opening your own bakery or restaurant?

Yeah, I've thought about it, but for now, I'm just focusing on my YouTube channel and learning new skills. Maybe someday I'll make the leap and open my own place, but for now, I'm happy with my current job and hobbies.
That makes sense. It's always good to have goals and dreams, but it's also important to enjoy the present moment and appreciate what you have. Well, it was great chatting with you, Shiloh!
Yeah, thanks for the chat! I'm looking forward to my next video and maybe even doing a collaboration with some other beekeepers in the area.
Sounds like a plan! Good luck with your channel and your triathlon training. I'll be rooting for you on August 20th!
